content: Examination of the 2017 Will (E1): 1. Paper: Spectral analysis confirms the paper is from a batch manufactured in January 2015, consistent with stationery found in Anil Chatterjee’s study. 2. Ink: Chromatography matches Parker Pen Model 51, purchased by Priya Chatterjee on 05/03/2017 from a local stationery shop in Kharagpur (receipt attached). 3. Signature: Digital enhancement reveals tracing patterns consistent with use of a lightbox — the signature is a trace of a prior genuine signature, not an original. 4. Notary Seal: The seal impression matches a stolen stamp from 'Roy & Associates,' a law firm that ceased operations in 2016. The notary's signature is a photocopied forgery. Conclusion: The 2017 will is a forged document, created after 05/03/2017 using paper from 2015 and ink from a pen purchased by Priya Chatterjee.
